name: TAO 4h Compression Fakeout Re-Expansion v1
edge_mechanism: compression resolves with a fake move one way, then directional confirmation appears the other way and the real expansion is entered
thesis: Cycle 71 includes compression fakeout into re-expansion with directional confirmation. TAO 4h is the best-fit structurally different lane because its trend contamination gives real room for the second move to run once the fake side fails. The edge is not raw squeeze breakout. It is identifying the fake release and entering the real expansion with confirmation.
invalidation_condition: If TAO 4h compressions are not followed by tradable fakeout-and-go sequences, or if the directional confirmation is too late and sparse, the thesis fails.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Volatility compresses, one side breaks but fails, the opposite side gains directional confirmation, and a stronger expansion follows.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Compression frequently traps premature breakout traders. On a structurally stronger asset like TAO, the second move after the fakeout can carry better than on flat majors.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: TAO 4h is the best fit because it is the only basket lane with enough live trend contamination to plausibly reward re-expansion after a fake release.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: The fakeout may not be distinct enough, turning the setup into either late breakout chasing or under-trading.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If the fakeout reads correctly but profits are truncated, keep the trigger and extend runner management before widening compression thresholds.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Lower count than SOL/ETH chop reversals, but stronger asymmetry when the re-expansion catches a real directional leg.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: TAO 4h clears density and at least one cross-lane shows a weaker but visible fakeout-to-go pattern.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-12 trades, or fakeout-confirmation entries that reverse immediately and never expand.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: moderate but acceptable because compression plus fakeout occurs more often on volatile TAO than on majors

validation_basket.why_included: Best-fit lane for fakeout into genuine re-expansion because the second move has room to run.
validation_basket.continue_if: TAO 4h produces enough fakeout-confirmed expansions with positive first-target behavior.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: TAO 4h either under-trades or fakeouts fail to convert into real expansions.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Moderate count with a minority of strong runners carrying the expectancy.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same fakeout-and-go logic works on the cycle primary asset under milder trend pressure.
validation_basket.continue_if: SOL 4h retains the fakeout-reexpansion grammar with enough density.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: SOL 4h proves the concept depends too heavily on TAO's trendiness.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Cleaner but smaller second-leg expansions than TAO.

validation_basket.why_included: Useful control to see if a pure chop lane suppresses the second-leg expansion too much.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH 4h still prints some fakeout-to-go sequences with structure.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: ETH 4h is too flat and confirms that the thesis needs trend contamination.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Sparse but orderly fakeout reversals without big carry.

long: Time-based de-risk: cut 20% more after 6 bars if no 1.1 ATR expansion
short: Time-based de-risk: cut 20% more after 6 bars if no 1.1 ATR expansion
